proposed_new_file.content:
SOURCE: https://john09289.github.io/predictions/wins.html
TITLE: Confirmed Wins (69) — Ovoid Cavity Cosmological Model V51.1
EXTRACTED: 2026-05-08
PAGE_HASH_MD5: 16d65a42722d2d5869842d14c8f699f0 (91,686 bytes; matches poller last-recorded hash, byte-identical for 34 consecutive polls through 2026-05-07T11:05:55Z)
PROVENANCE_NOTE: Captured to verify two V51-era quotes cited in data/wins.json WIN-064 detail_extra (post-EXP-200 paragraph). The pre-V51 archive raw-text/02-wins.txt is dated 2026-03-12 and predates WIN-064. This file captures the V51 WIN-064 entry verbatim plus its surrounding context (WIN-063 and WIN-065 headers) for orientation. EXP-293 / ISS-1332 / WIN-064.c5.json hole #2.
SECURITY: CLEAN — no prompt injection, no hidden text, no malicious code. Page is the same byte-identical wins.html surface the poller has been reading for ~17 days (34 consecutive byte-identical polls).
WAYBACK_NOTE: archive.org/wayback/available API returned zero archived snapshots for this URL on 2026-05-08, so a Wayback citation (curmudgeon Hole #2 option (a)) is unavailable; this raw-text capture (option (b)) is the substitute.
===

[Surrounding context — WIN-063 trailing paragraph]

  Dome vs Globe: A symmetric globe dipole requires approximately equal decay rates at conjugate latitudes (~1.0x ratio). A 3:1 asymmetry is structurally incompatible with symmetric core flow. The Toroidal Ovoid predicts amplified southern decay because the sub-terrestrial return path exits preferentially through the SAA shoulder.

=== WIN-064 (VERBATIM, wins.html lines 838-849) ===

<!-- WIN-064 -->
WIN-064 — Seismology / Structure — 2026-04-04
WIN-064 — P-Wave Geometric Lock: 104°–140° Shadow Zone   [CONFIRMED 2026-04-04]

What we predicted: The Sub-Terrestrial Wall of the Closed Toroidal Ovoid creates a physical P-wave shadow zone between 104° and 140° angular distance from any seismic source, corresponding to the toroidal return-path boundary.

What the data returned: USGS seismological records confirm the P-wave shadow zone at exactly 104°–140°. No direct P-wave arrivals are observed within this angular band. → WIN-064

Dome vs Globe: Globe attributes the shadow zone to refraction through a liquid outer core. The V51.0 Toroidal model attributes it to a physical structural boundary — the Sub-Terrestrial Wall where the aetheric return path meets the bottom plate of the ovoid cavity.

=== END WIN-064 BLOCK ===

[Surrounding context — WIN-065 header for orientation]

WIN-065 — Astronomy / Positional — 2026-04-04
WIN-065 — Polaris Systematic Excess: +0.27° at 35.9°N   [CONFIRMED 2026-04-04]
What we predicted: The H(r) exponential height function predicts a systematic positive excess in Polaris elevation above geographic latitude, increasing with the curvature of the exponential dome at mid-latitudes.

===

CITED-QUOTE INDEX (for fast lookup from data/wins.json WIN-064 detail_extra):
- Quote A (mechanism verb "creates"):
    "The Sub-Terrestrial Wall of the Closed Toroidal Ovoid creates a physical P-wave shadow zone between 104° and 140° angular distance from any seismic source, corresponding to the toroidal return-path boundary."
    LOCATION: "What we predicted" line of WIN-064 entry above. Exact verbatim match including em-dash, degree symbols, and en-dash range separator.
- Quote B ("bottom plate of the ovoid cavity"):
    "a physical structural boundary — the Sub-Terrestrial Wall where the aetheric return path meets the bottom plate of the ovoid cavity"
    LOCATION: "Dome vs Globe" line of WIN-064 entry above. Exact verbatim match. (The detail_extra paraphrase shortens the lead-in to "a physical structural boundary … where the aetheric return path meets the bottom plate of the ovoid cavity" using ellipsis for elision; the source text reads in full as preserved here.)

VERIFICATION: Run `grep -F "creates a physical P-wave shadow zone" raw-text/02b-wins-V51-win064.txt` and `grep -F "bottom plate of the ovoid cavity" raw-text/02b-wins-V51-win064.txt` — both should return exactly one match each, confirming archival presence of both quoted phrases that EXP-200 added to WIN-064 detail_extra.
proposed_new_file.out_of_scope: This file captures only the WIN-064 region. The broader infrastructure gap — that ALL V51-era wins (WIN-040..WIN-069 plus the V51.1 register) live only on the live site with no full repo archive — is NOT closed by this expansion. A separate infrastructure EXP would propose a full raw-text/02-wins-V51.txt covering all V51 wins, plus a poller change to auto-snapshot V51-era pages going forward. Logged here as out-of-scope follow-up; this expansion stays narrowly scoped to the WIN-064 / EXP-200 citation exposure that c5 hole #2 specifically flagged.

proposed_text_changes.old_text: the Dome-vs-Globe row explicitly re-asserts the mechanism as <q>a physical structural boundary … where the aetheric return path meets the bottom plate of the ovoid cavity</q>.
proposed_text_changes.new_text: the Dome-vs-Globe row explicitly re-asserts the mechanism as a physical structural boundary … where the aetheric return path meets the bottom plate of the ovoid cavity [both quotes archived verbatim at raw-text/02b-wins-V51-win064.txt, captured 2026-05-08; live page MD5 16d65a42722d2d5869842d14c8f699f0 has been byte-identical across 34 consecutive poller checks (~17 days through 2026-05-07T11:05:55Z), so the citation is auditable against both our archive and the dome's currently-served page].
proposed_text_changes.rationale: Adds an archival-citation annotation after the two quoted strings (which sit in the same sentence inside the 'Closing the non-incompatibility retreat' paragraph). The annotation cites the new raw-text/02b-wins-V51-win064.txt archive AND the live-page MD5 corroborated by 34 consecutive byte-identical poller hashes. Both halves of the citation are independently auditable: a third-party reader can grep the archive file in our repo, AND can md5sum the live page to confirm the hash quoted in the annotation matches what we observed. The archive-cite span class is consistent with the docs/index.html .archive-cite styling (small inline note, distinct from main prose).

holes_addressed: WIN-064.c5 Hole #2 (major): 'Raw-text archive does not contain the two dome quotes cited in the new paragraph'. CLOSED — both quotes now archived verbatim in raw-text/02b-wins-V51-win064.txt, plus inline citation in detail_extra makes the verification chain visible in the prose.
holes_not_addressed: Hole #1 (major, 'every other N/A-tolerance domain' over-reach): Already addressed in EXP-200 follow-on (data/wins.json now reads 'alongside any other N/A-tolerance domain that similarly lacks a threshold or categorical criterion (threshold-style panels such as Anomaly >2σ are not affected by this critique)' — narrowed per curmudgeon recommendation; CMB Axis of Evil counter-example pre-empted). Verified in current WIN-064 detail_extra read 2026-05-08.
Hole #3 (moderate, redundant 'preempt' label): Cosmetic; deferred to a separate light-edit pass — not in scope for the citation-verification fix.

Hole #4 (moderate, categorical-binary third horn): Already addressed in current WIN-064 detail_extra — final paragraph closes with 'A third reading — that WIN-064 records a categorical binary test (shadow present or absent in the 104°–140° band) — fails on the same geometry: a fixed Sub-Terrestrial Wall occupies a specific arc position relative to the disc centre, producing a directionally biased shadow, not the omnidirectional 104°–140° ring observed symmetrically around every epicenter worldwide.' Verified verbatim in current detail_extra; hole #4 already closed.
Hole #5 (minor, TLDR doesn't mention non-incompatibility argument): Skippable per curmudgeon. Not touched by this expansion.
new_evidence_added: raw-text/02b-wins-V51-win064.txt — verbatim WIN-064 capture from wins.html dated 2026-05-08, MD5 16d65a42722d2d5869842d14c8f699f0

Wayback Machine API confirmation: zero archived snapshots of the dome's wins.html (per archive.org/wayback/available query 2026-05-08) — establishes that option (a) of curmudgeon Hole #2 was genuinely unavailable, not skipped

Poller hash continuity: 34 consecutive byte-identical poller checks of wins.html through 2026-05-07T11:05:55Z (per monitor/status.json), establishing that the page state we archived is the same state EXP-200 was authored against (EXP-200 dated 2026-04-21, ~17 days before this archive)

anticipated_objections.objection: A defender argues: 'You captured the page on 2026-05-08, but EXP-200 was integrated 2026-04-21. Maybe the dome author edited wins.html between those dates and you can't tell the difference.'
anticipated_objections.response: The poller has hashed wins.html at every cycle (12-hour cadence) since well before 2026-04-21 and recorded byte-identity across all 34 consecutive polls through 2026-05-07T11:05:55Z. status.json's poll note explicitly logs '34th consecutive byte-identical predictions.html / ~18.46d' and its sibling-page tracking shows the wins MD5 unchanged at 16d65a42... across the same window. So the 2026-05-08 capture matches the 2026-04-21 EXP-200-authoring state by transitivity through the poller record. If the dome author silently edits between two polls, the next poll would change the hash — and that hasn't happened on the wins surface in over two weeks.

anticipated_objections.objection: A defender argues: 'The archive is in your repo. You could have edited it after the fact to make the citations look right.'
anticipated_objections.response: The git history is visible. The new raw-text file is added in a commit dated 2026-05-08; the citation annotation in detail_extra is added in the same commit. A reader can replay the commit, run an independent md5sum of the live page, and verify the recorded hash matches. The hash is the audit anchor — not the file content. Anyone can fake file content, but only the dome author can fake the live-page hash; we did not, and the poller's recorded hash chain (34 consecutive byte-identical checks) corroborates this.

anticipated_objections.objection: A defender argues: 'You only archived the WIN-064 region, not the whole page. How do I know you didn't selectively quote out of context?'
anticipated_objections.response: The WIN-064 entry on wins.html is a self-contained <div class='win confirmed' id='win-064'> block (HTML lines 838-849); the surrounding context (WIN-063 trailing paragraph and WIN-065 leading paragraph) is also captured in the archive for orientation. The cited quotes are atomic statements within the block — there is no plausible meta-context that could change their meaning. If a reader wants the full 91 KB of wins.html, they can md5sum it themselves against the archive's recorded hash; the page is still publicly served at the cited URL.

kernel_of_truth: The dome author has, in fact, written the cited quotes verbatim on the live site. EXP-200's citation work was correct — the only gap was archival auditability against a possible future edit. Curmudgeon Hole #2 is well-taken: a citation that lives only on a mutable surface is not a citation a hostile reviewer can verify. This expansion closes that gap by anchoring the citation to (a) our own archive copy and (b) a hash-corroborated reference to the live page state at the time of authoring. It does NOT improve the substantive argument — that work was already done in EXP-200.
out_of_scope_followup: Full V51 wins archive: raw-text/02-wins-V51.txt covering all V51 wins (WIN-040..WIN-069 plus front-matter), to close the broader exposure that any V51-era citation has a similar verifiability gap. Logged for a future infrastructure EXP, not in scope here.

Poller change: extend monitor.py to auto-snapshot V51-era pages (write a versioned raw-text capture on every poll where the version string changes, so future V52 transitions get archival continuity automatically). Logged for tinker; not in scope for the analyst Mode 1 expansion procedure.
